findings.summary: The reporting/ package conflates three distinct responsibilities — HTML generation, test-result persistence, and pipeline orchestration — within a single flat package, with no sub-grouping by concern
evidence: pipeline_reporter.py lines 226-384: embeds a full HTML renderer (generate_html_report) with inline CSS alongside pipeline data assembly, blurring the line between data and presentation
suite_summary_generator.py lines 358-411: run_test_summary_generation() writes to disk and prints console output — an orchestration concern — inside a module whose declared role is report generation

dashboard_generator.py: separately generates matplotlib charts and HTML templates, yet lives at the same flat level as text report generators, giving the package two incompatible rendering paradigms with no grouping
findings.suggestion: Split infrastructure/reporting/ into sub-packages: data/ (parsers, aggregators), renderers/ (HTML, markdown, charts), and runner/ (orchestration entry points). This makes ownership boundaries predictable and allows each concern to evolve independently.

findings.summary: PipelineExecutor._run_infrastructure_tests is a single-line private wrapper that adds no policy, only calling run_infrastructure_tests(), which itself delegates immediately to _run_script()
evidence: pipeline.py line 396-398: _run_infrastructure_tests delegates unconditionally to run_infrastructure_tests(), which on line 392-394 delegates unconditionally to _run_script(). Three hops, zero added policy at any level.

The public run_infrastructure_tests() exists solely for the multi-project orchestrator, but the private _run_infrastructure_tests() is then only used internally by _build_stage_list, making one of the two names redundant.
findings.suggestion: Remove _run_infrastructure_tests and register run_infrastructure_tests directly in _build_stage_list. If the public name must exist for external callers, make it the only entry point and reference it directly in _build_stage_list — eliminating the indirection layer.

findings.summary: In suite_runner.run_test_suite, the branch `if exit_code == 0: pass` is dead control flow that adds noise without behavior
evidence: suite_runner.py lines 286-292: `if exit_code == 0: pass` — the pass branch does nothing and exists only to anchor the elif/else chain. The comment '# Caller logs success' documents the absence of action, not an intent that requires a branch.
findings.suggestion: Remove the `if exit_code == 0: pass` arm entirely and invert the logic: `if exit_code != 0:` containing the error/warning branches. This reduces nesting and makes the control flow direct.

findings.summary: pipeline_reporter.py mixes high-abstraction report assembly with low-abstraction inline HTML string building in the same module, and duplicates the section-rendering pattern (test, validation, performance, error, output) across both generate_html_report and _generate_pipeline_markdown without a shared iteration contract
evidence: pipeline_reporter.py lines 125-223 and 226-384: both _generate_pipeline_markdown and generate_html_report independently iterate over the same optional sections (test_results, validation_results, performance_metrics, error_summary, output_statistics) with near-identical guard logic (if report.X:) and field lookups. Adding a new section requires editing both functions.

generate_html_report (line 226) builds multi-hundred-line HTML strings with f-string concatenation inline with the section-rendering logic, mixing template concerns with data-access concerns in a single function body.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared section descriptor list (name, attribute, render_fn) iterated by both renderers, so adding a new pipeline report section requires one addition instead of two. Alternatively, move HTML generation to infrastructure/reporting/html_templates.py (which already exists) so pipeline_reporter.py contains only data assembly.
